title: galium pumilum subsp. vulgatum
intro: Galium pumilum subsp. vulgatum, also known as the dwarf bedstraw, is a sub-species of the Galium pumilum plant, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive problems, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Galium pumilum subsp. vulgatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to address various health concerns.
